title: Stenogrammitis ascensionensis
taxonomy: Stenogrammitis ascensionensis belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This species is further classified within the genus Stenogrammitis, which is a genus of epiphytic ferns. The kingdom it falls under is Plantae, which includes all plants. The order of classification for Stenogrammitis ascensionensis is Polypodiales, which is an order of ferns. Its classification also includes the division Pteridophyta, which is a division of vascular plants without seeds.
uses: Stenogrammitis ascensionensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Ascension Island, where it is believed to possess antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including skin infections and respiratory issues. In some regions, the leaves are crushed and applied topically to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Furthermore, Stenogrammitis ascensionensis is also used as a treatment for fever and digestive problems. However, its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific study and verification.
